title: Quiz: Approximate QFT
description: Test your understanding of QFT approximation techniques

questions.question: Why do we need approximate QFT in practice?

options.text: Small rotation angles are difficult to implement precisely on hardware

questions.explanation: Exact QFT requires exponentially small rotations (2π/2ⁿ) that hardware cannot implement precisely. Truncating these reduces errors from imprecise gates.

questions.question: In approximate QFT with cutoff m, which gates are kept?

options.text: All R_k gates where k > m
All R_k gates where k ≤ m

questions.explanation: We keep R_k gates with k ≤ m (larger rotations) and discard those with k > m (negligibly small rotations).

questions.question: How does the gate count scale for approximate QFT with fixed cutoff m?

options.text: O(n·m) which is O(n) for fixed m

questions.explanation: With cutoff m, we have O(n·m) controlled rotations. For m = O(log n), this gives O(n log n) total.

questions.question: What cutoff m is typically sufficient for bounded error?
questions.explanation: m = O(log n) provides enough precision for most applications while dramatically reducing gate count.

questions.question: The approximate QFT is essential for practical quantum computing on NISQ devices.
questions.explanation: NISQ devices have limited coherence times and gate fidelities. Reduced circuit depth from approximate QFT is critical for practical implementation.
